title: Android Device Control (ADB)

use_cases.name: Dump UI Hierarchy
use_cases.description: Dump the current Android UI XML hierarchy for inspection
methods: ui(keyword=None, clickable_only=False, raw=False) — top-level entry point
_dump_u2() — connects via uiautomator2, calls d.dump_hierarchy(), no idle needed
_dump_native() — runs 'adb shell uiautomator dump --compressed /sdcard/ui.xml', pulls file via 'adb pull'
use_cases.fallback: u2 is tried first; if it fails or returns <100 chars, falls back to native ADB dump

use_cases.name: Parse & Filter UI Nodes
use_cases.description: Parse raw XML into structured node list with text, coordinates, clickable flag, class, resource-id
methods: _parse_xml(xml_str, keyword=None, clickable_only=False, raw=False)
Iterates all <node> elements via ET.fromstring + root.iter('node')
Filters: skip 'termux' packages, skip nodes without label unless raw=True
Computes center coords (cx, cy) from bounds=[x1,y1][x2,y2]
Returns list of dicts: {text, click, edit, cx, cy, cls, rid}

use_cases.name: Tap on Screen
use_cases.description: Send a tap gesture to specific screen coordinates on the Android device
methods: tap(x, y) — runs 'adb shell input tap x y'
use_cases.notes: Uses physical pixel coordinates derived from UI node bounds

use_cases.name: Swipe on Screen
use_cases.description: Send a swipe gesture on the Android device
methods: Performed via ADB: 'adb shell input swipe x1 y1 x2 y2 [duration]'
Not explicitly defined as a function in adb_ui.py — called directly via subprocess or via u2
use_cases.notes: Implied by scope; typically adb shell input swipe or u2 swipe

use_cases.name: Input Text
use_cases.description: Type text into a focused EditText field on the Android device
methods: Performed via ADB: 'adb shell input text <string>'
EditText nodes flagged with edit=True in parsed node list (cls=='EditText')
Not explicitly defined as a function in adb_ui.py — called directly
use_cases.notes: Agent identifies EditText nodes from UI dump, taps to focus, then sends input text

use_cases.name: Read Device State
use_cases.description: Query current UI state to determine what is on screen (clickable elements, text, layout)
methods: ui(clickable_only=True) — returns only clickable nodes
ui(keyword='X') — filter nodes by keyword
ui(raw=True) — return raw node list for programmatic use
Detects popups: full-screen FrameLayout + small bottom ImageView (close X)

use_cases.name: Windows Desktop Interaction (ljqCtrl)
use_cases.description: Control Windows desktop: mouse click, keyboard input, image template matching
methods: Click(x, y) / click(x, y) — physical coords, calls SetCursorPos + MouseClick
SetCursorPos(z) — scales logical→physical via dpi_scale, calls win32api.SetCursorPos
MouseClick(staytime=0.05) — MOUSEEVENTF_LEFTDOWN + UP
MouseDClick(staytime=0.05) — double click
Press(cmd, staytime=0) — keyboard shortcut via win32api.keybd_event + VK_CODE map
FindBlock(fn, wrect=None, threshold=0.8) — cv2.matchTemplate screenshot search → (center_phys, is_found)
GrabWindow(hwnd) — DPI-safe window screenshot via ImageGrab
GetWRect(sr) — compute region rect from string ('left2', 'bottom3', etc.)
use_cases.notes: ljqCtrl is Windows-only (win32api). CRITICAL: do NOT import pyautogui alongside this module. dpi_scale = logical_width / physical_width

key_relationships: Agent uses adb_ui.ui() as primary Android interface
adb_ui.ui() includes _dump_u2() (preferred, no idle needed) with fallback to _dump_native()
_dump_native() uses subprocess ADB commands + file pull to local ui_mt.xml
_parse_xml() is called by ui() to convert raw XML to structured nodes
adb_ui.tap() sends ADB shell input tap command
ljqCtrl provides Windows-side mouse/keyboard automation (separate from ADB)
Agent may use both adb_ui (Android) and ljqCtrl (Windows host) in parallel automation
important_notes: u2 (uiautomator2) is preferred over native dump — works even when device is not idle (e.g. animations)
Native dump requires device idle state; uses 'uiautomator dump --compressed' via ADB shell
Termux package nodes are always filtered out from UI results
swipe and input_text are not wrapped as named functions in adb_ui.py — used as raw ADB commands
